expectations.evidence: 'Verdict: NOT READY FOR DEVELOPMENT' and 'This spec requires substantial additional detail'
Asks probing questions ('Which fields specifically?', 'Where does this UI live?') but never explicitly flags that tasks lack file paths as a structural problem.
'What form controls are needed?', 'What happens on save?', 'Which email service?'
Lists 7 missing ACs: default preferences, mandatory notifications, persistence, email service respects prefs, partial opt-out, error handling, audit.
'"Write some tests" is not actionable. A ready-for-dev spec should specify: Unit tests, Integration tests, UI tests, Edge cases'

Uses own categories: 'Task definitions', 'Acceptance criteria', 'Testing plan', 'Data model', 'API contract', etc. Does not use the 5 standard criteria (Actionable, Logical, Testable, Complete, Self-Contained).
7 numbered recommendations covering data model, notification categories, ACs, API contract, testing plan, compliance, UI specs.
